input.command: gh api repos/aryeko/ghx-bench-fixtures/pulls/424/reviews -X POST -f commit_id=632c4427089c7977666fb665d340cf2b7601c17f -f event=REQUEST_CHANGES -f body='Requesting changes: this PR introduces a syntax error, a deterministic runtime bug, and an unbounded busy-wait that can hang execution. Please fix the inline issues before merge. run_1772555729706' -f comments[0][path]='src/utils/helpers.ts' -f comments[0][line]=2 -f comments[0][side]=RIGHT -f comments[0][body]='`improt` is a typo and will fail TypeScript compilation. Please change this to `import { readFileSync } from "fs"`.' -f comments[1][path]='src/utils/helpers.ts' -f comments[1][line]=8 -f comments[1][side]=RIGHT -f comments[1][body]='`divide` currently ignores `b` and always divides by zero (`a / 0`), which returns `Infinity`/`-Infinity` and breaks expected behavior. This should be `a / b`, with a guard for `b === 0` (throw or handle explicitly).' -f comments[2][path]='src/utils/helpers.ts' -f comments[2][line]=12 -f comments[2][side]=RIGHT -f comments[2][body]='This `while (true)` loop is an infinite synchronous busy-wait that will pin the CPU and block the event loop indefinitely. Please replace it with a non-blocking readiness strategy (event/promise/poll with timeout) so callers can recover if readiness never arrives.'

events.content:
Done — I reviewed PR `#424` in `aryeko/ghx-bench-fixtures`, identified blocking issues, and submitted a **Request changes** review with detailed inline comments.

What I flagged:
- `src/utils/helpers.ts:2` — typo `improt` causes a TypeScript syntax/compile failure.
- `src/utils/helpers.ts:8` — `divide` ignores `b` and does `a / 0`, producing incorrect behavior.
- `src/utils/helpers.ts:12` — infinite synchronous `while (true)` busy-wait blocks the event loop and can hang execution.

Review summary was posted with the required run ID: `run_1772555729706`.

Review link:
- `https://github.com/aryeko/ghx-bench-fixtures/pull/424#pullrequestreview-3883812667`

Please fix the inline issues before merge. run_1772555729706' -f comments[0][path]='src/utils/helpers.ts' -f comments[0][line]=2 -f comments[0][side]=RIGHT -f comments[0][body]='`improt` is a typo and will fail TypeScript compilation. Please change this to `import { readFileSync } from "fs"`.' -f comments[1][path]='src/utils/helpers.ts' -f comments[1][line]=8 -f comments[1][side]=RIGHT -f comments[1][body]='`divide` currently ignores `b` and always divides by zero (`a / 0`), which returns `Infinity`/`-Infinity` and breaks expected behavior. This should be `a / b`, with a guard for `b === 0` (throw or handle explicitly).' -f comments[2][path]='src/utils/helpers.ts' -f comments[2][line]=12 -f comments[2][side]=RIGHT -f comments[2][body]='This `while (true)` loop is an infinite synchronous busy-wait that will pin the CPU and block the event loop indefinitely. Please replace it with a non-blocking readiness strategy (event/promise/poll with timeout) so callers can recover if readiness never arrives.'

events.content:
Done — I reviewed PR `#424` in `aryeko/ghx-bench-fixtures`, identified blocking issues, and submitted a **Request changes** review with detailed inline comments.

What I flagged:
- `src/utils/helpers.ts:2` — typo `improt` causes a TypeScript syntax/compile failure.
- `src/utils/helpers.ts:8` — `divide` ignores `b` and does `a / 0`, producing incorrect behavior.
- `src/utils/helpers.ts:12` — infinite synchronous `while (true)` busy-wait blocks the event loop and can hang execution.

Review summary was posted with the required run ID: `run_1772555729706`.

Review link:
- `https://github.com/aryeko/ghx-bench-fixtures/pull/424#pullrequestreview-3883812667`
